report_name: Over 200,000 Scam Sites Powered by Legitimate Chinese DCloud Framework, Infoblox Reports

threat_actors.name: DCloud Framework Abusing Threat Actors
threat_actors.description: A collective of numerous threat actors and groups leveraging the legitimate DCloud Uni-App framework to create and operate over 200,000 fraudulent websites for financial gain. They engage in investment scams, pig-butchering, and brand impersonation on a global scale.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Used to conduct brand impersonation, investment scams, and 'pig-butchering' romance scams on a massive scale through over 200,000 fraudulent websites and applications built with the DCloud Uni-App framework.

attack_patterns.name: Impersonation
attack_patterns.description: Threat actors created phishing sites that mimic legitimate companies to steal credentials or financial information as part of a massive fraud operation.

attack_patterns.name: Acquire Infrastructure: Domains
attack_patterns.description: Threat actors registered over 236,000 second-level domains to host fraudulent websites, with some registrations suggesting a coordinated infrastructure-as-a-service model.

attack_patterns.name: Obtain Capabilities: Tool
attack_patterns.description: Scammers purchase pre-made scam templates built with the DCloud Uni-App framework from an underground market, allowing for rapid deployment of fraudulent campaigns.

tools.name: DCloud Uni-App
tools.description: A legitimate Chinese open-source development framework abused by threat actors to rapidly build and deploy cross-platform fraudulent websites and applications for various scams.

campaigns.name: RainbowEx Scam
campaigns.description: A fake cryptocurrency exchange and investment fraud scheme built using the DCloud Uni-App framework.
campaigns.objective: Financial fraud via fake cryptocurrency exchange.

campaigns.name: Lightning Shared Scooter Co. (LSSC) Scam
campaigns.description: An elaborate investment fraud that set up physical storefronts in the U.S. to appear legitimate while defrauding investors of millions, using applications built with the DCloud Uni-App framework.

campaigns.name: Yuechi Sharing Technology Ltd. (YST) Operation
campaigns.description: A large-scale scam operation where the Uni-App frontend connects to a shared backend network, indicative of a 'Scam-as-a-Service' model.
campaigns.objective: Financial fraud through a Scam-as-a-Service model.

infrastructure.name: DCloud Scam-as-a-Service Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: A massive network of over 236,000 fraudulent domains and shared backend servers used to host scam websites built with the DCloud Uni-App framework. This infrastructure supports a 'Scam-as-a-Service' model for financial fraud.

indicators.name: DCloud Framework URL Artifact
indicators.pattern:
title: DCloud Framework URL Artifact
id: 9c5b1a3d-8f7e-4b1c-9d5a-6b8c0f1e2d3a
status: experimental
description: Detects a URL path structure commonly associated with applications built using the DCloud Uni-App framework, which has been abused for large-scale scam operations.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/chinese-framework-powers-200000-scam-sites/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-28
tags:
  - attack.t1566
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains: '/unpackage/dist/dev/mp-weixin/'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects a URL path structure (`/unpackage/dist/dev/mp-weixin/`) commonly associated with applications built using the DCloud Uni-App framework, which is heavily abused for scam websites.

indicators.name: Suspicious TLDs Associated with DCloud Scams
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious TLDs Associated with DCloud Scams
id: f1e2d3c4-b5a6-4987-8fed-cba987654321
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to TLDs (.top, .xyz, .online) commonly used in the DCloud framework scam campaigns.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/chinese-framework-powers-200000-scam-sites/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-28
tags:
  - attack.t1583.001
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of these TLDs.
level: low
logsource:
  category: dns_query
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|endswith:
      - '.top'
      - '.xyz'
      - '.online'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects DNS queries for domains ending in .top, .xyz, or .online, which are TLDs commonly used by the scam websites mentioned in the report.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious TLDs Associated with DCloud Scams
